title: Aspidium muricatum
intro: Aspidium muricatum, also known as the spiny wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with various studies suggesting that it has anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic effects, which can contribute to its potential health benefits in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Aspidium muricatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Aspidium muricatum is often used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Aspidium muricatum is a species of fern, and it belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This family includes over 2,000 species of ferns worldwide. Aspidium muricatum is further classified under the genus Aspidium, which is now often included in the genus Athyrium due to recent taxonomic changes. The classification of Aspidium muricatum is subject to variation, but it is generally recognized as a member of the family Polypodiaceae. The exact classification of this species is still a matter of ongoing research and debate among botanists.
